# CLASI Software Engineering Process

This project uses the CLASI SE process. **You are the CLASI team-lead** — the root agent the user interacts with. Read `.claude/agents/team-lead/agent.md` at session start for your role and workflow. Do NOT spawn or dispatch a sub-agent for orchestration; you ARE the team-lead, and you orchestrate sprint-planner and programmer sub-agents yourself per that role definition.

<!-- Generated by clasi 0.20260722.1 -->
<!-- CLASI:END -->

# Coding Standards

**C++ code in this project follows the
[Google C++ Style Guide](https://google.github.io/styleguide/cppguide.html).**
All agents writing or reviewing C++ (firmware, wedgelab, source/, tests) must
conform to it — naming, formatting, header layout, and idiom. When editing
existing code that predates this rule, bring the touched code into conformance
rather than matching the old style.

**The operative reference is the condensed guide at
`docs/reference/google-cppguide-condensed.md`** — project overrides already
applied inline; read it, not the full vendored HTML. Project overrides take
precedence over the Google guide where they conflict — see
`.claude/rules/naming-and-style.md` and `.claude/rules/coding-standards.md`.
Notably: CamelCase naming — UpperCamelCase for class/struct/
protocol/namespace names (acronyms fully capitalized), lowerCamelCase for
variable and function names (function names NEVER start with an uppercase
letter; we do not use Google's PascalCase functions) — and units never appear
in any identifier (fields, methods, parameters) — units go in `// [unit]`
comment tags.

# Project Documentation — READ FIRST

**All robot/firmware/relay/radio/protocol/tooling documentation lives at
https://robots.jointheleague.org/ — consult it FIRST (WebFetch) for anything you
don't already know about the hardware or wire protocol, BEFORE reverse-engineering
behavior or writing diagnostic code.**

Hard-won technical knowledge from debugging sessions is captured in
[`.clasi/knowledge/`](.clasi/knowledge/) (e.g. the radio-relay `!GO` data-plane comms
protocol — the host must open the relay with DTR asserted, send `!GO` to enter the
data plane, then send plain commands with no `>` prefix; `rogo`/`robot_radio` use
the older `>`-prefix protocol and cannot reach the robot through the current relay
firmware). Check there before re-deriving comms or hardware behavior.
